question: How does Novo stack up vs Eli Lilly in the GLP-1 race, and is the 3x valuation gap justified?

findings.claim: SURMOUNT-5 head-to-head trial (NEJM, May 2025): Tirzepatide achieved 20.2% weight loss vs semaglutide's 13.7% — 47% greater relative efficacy
findings.data: Phase 3b, open-label, 751 participants randomized 1:1. Tirzepatide max tolerated dose (10/15mg) vs semaglutide max dose (1.7/2.4mg) for 72 weeks. Tirzepatide: -20.2% body weight (LS mean), -50.3 lbs absolute. Semaglutide: -13.7%, -33.1 lbs. 31.6% of tirzepatide patients lost >25% vs 16.1% semaglutide. P<0.001. Funded by Eli Lilly. NCT05822830.

findings.claim: CagriSema (Novo's next-gen) FAILED non-inferiority vs tirzepatide in REDEFINE 4 trial (Feb 2026): 20.2% vs 23.6% weight loss
findings.data: REDEFINE 4 trial: CagriSema did NOT achieve primary endpoint of non-inferiority to tirzepatide at 84 weeks. CagriSema: 20.2% weight loss, tirzepatide: 23.6%. NVO stock fell 16% on the news (Feb 23, 2026). Barclays cut CagriSema peak sales estimate from $12B to $2B (>80% reduction). JPMorgan: results 'will make it difficult for the product to gain market share.' Morningstar cut NVO fair value from DKK 372 to DKK 343.

findings.claim: Earlier CagriSema disappointment (Dec 2024): Only 22.7% weight loss vs 25% target, with only 57.3% of patients on full dose
findings.data: CagriSema Phase 3 initial readout: 22.7% average weight loss, below 25% target. Only 57.3% of participants took full dose throughout trial, potentially explaining the miss. NVO lost 30% of value in 6 months following this result. CagriSema filed with FDA Dec 2025; decision expected late 2026.

findings.claim: Meta-analysis confirms tirzepatide superiority: mean difference of 4.23 percentage points more weight loss vs semaglutide across clinical and real-world data
findings.data: Systematic review and meta-analysis of clinical trials + real-world data. Tirzepatide MD = 4.23 percentage points more weight loss (95% CI: 3.22-5.25, P<0.01) vs semaglutide. Dose- and duration-dependent effect.
